page: Google Cloud Platform c4n-standard-96 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the c4n server family. It features 96 dedicated virtual CPUs and 360.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 3.75 GB of RAM per virtual core. This dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution performance without resource contention from other tenant workloads.

This server configuration does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external networking configurations for public internet access.

Given the high core count of 96 dedicated vCPUs paired with 360.0 GB of RAM, the c4n-standard-96 is designed for compute-heavy workloads that require substantial parallel processing capabilities. It is suited for tasks such as high-performance web serving, batch processing, continuous integration and continuous delivery pipelines, and distributed application hosting.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-standard-96 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4n family, configured with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 360.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a resource ratio of 3.75 GB of memory per virtual core. The instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPU accelerators, requiring external network-attached storage for data persistence. With its high count of dedicated virtual processors, the c4n-standard-96 is engineered for parallel compute workloads, including large-scale web serving, CI/CD pipelines, and distributed application deployments. The dedicated CPU allocation guarantees stable performance by eliminating resource sharing with other tenants.
